_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to characterize network traffic for trends and patterns.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-004 (DA, cosine: 0.391), AI-K-007 (AI, cosine: 0.386).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.2/10 (raw 0.522). Shared vocabulary: [network]. DCWF-only terms [ability, characterize, patterns, traffic, trends] vs WIDAI-only terms [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, data, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.887, R=0.848). P exceeds R by 0.039 — the DCWF element's 9 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 31 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Ability to characterize network traffic for trends and patterns." and "Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.467 < STS 0.522 (gap -0.055). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [ability, characterize, patterns] and [affects, architecture, balancing] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.3%. Shared tokens: [network]. DCWF-only: [ability, characterize, patterns, traffic, trends]. WIDAI-only: [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, data, design].
